page_content: ENES499 Senior Projects in Engineering (3 Credits) Students will work in large teams to solve a multidisciplinary research/ design problem. The course will begin with students identifying opportunities, brainstorming project concepts to address these opportunities, applying lean startup and design thinking strategies, and then selecting/proposing a project for the semester. Acceptable projects will require the multidisciplinary design, construction and testing of a project within limited budget and time constraints. Prerequisite: Permission of ENGR-A. James Clark School of Engineering; and completion of all 1XX and 2xx level (lower-division) technical courses in engineering major with a C- or better. Restriction: Must be in a major in ENGR-A. James Clark School of Engineering. Repeatable to: 6 credits if content differs.
